chat.title: Chat
chat.subtitle: Quick questions about my stack
chat.welcome: Hi! Pick a question below and I’ll answer with details based on my stacks/projects.

questions.stack: What technologies do you use most?
questions.backend: How do you build a Node.js API?
questions.database: What do you use for databases?
questions.best_practices: What best practices do you apply on projects?
questions.clean_code: How do you keep code clean and maintainable?
questions.testing: Do you write tests? (unit / integration / e2e)
questions.code_review: How do you handle reviews and code quality?

answers.stack: My go-to stack is TypeScript end-to-end, React / Next.js for UI, Node.js for APIs, and PostgreSQL for data. I regularly use Tailwind CSS, Radix/shadcn components, and depending on the project: Prisma, Strapi, AWS (S3), and integrations like Stripe.
answers.frontend: On the frontend, I start with a small design system (tokens, reusable components), then build pages optimized for performance and conversion (SEO, accessibility, Core Web Vitals). I’m comfortable with Next.js (App Router) for SSR/SSG, i18n, routing, and clean UI animations with Tailwind.
answers.backend: With Node.js, I build REST or real-time APIs with a clear structure (validation, error handling, service layer, DB access). I focus on security (auth, permissions), observability (useful logs), and stable endpoints (versioning when needed).
answers.database: I work a lot with PostgreSQL and product-oriented schemas (relations, indexes, constraints). Depending on the context I use Prisma, and I take care of migrations and performance (queries, pagination, aggregations).
answers.realtime: Yes: I’ve implemented real-time features with WebSocket / Socket.IO for events (statuses, notifications, live data). Key points are reconnection, socket auth, and load limits.
answers.payments: Yes: Stripe integrations (checkout, webhooks, subscriptions) and also more local constraints depending on context (e.g. Fedapay). I secure webhooks and design reliable payment state machines (idempotency, retries).
answers.devops: Yes: I can handle deployment and production hardening (environments, variables, build, monitoring). I use Docker when it makes sense, and I can set up simple CI/CD (tests + lint + deploy).
answers.best_practices: I set a solid foundation early: strict TypeScript, lint/format, naming conventions, reusable components, clear separation of UI / business logic / data, and consistent error handling. I keep PRs small and readable, and write minimal documentation when it helps (setup, key decisions).
answers.clean_code: I aim for simple code: short functions, clear responsibilities, explicit types, no duplicated logic, and an architecture that follows the product. I keep dependencies under control and refactor when an area becomes hard to change.
answers.testing: Yes when it protects business outcomes: unit tests for core logic, integration tests for critical API/DB paths, and optionally e2e tests for key flows. The goal is to prevent regressions without slowing delivery.
answers.code_review: Even solo, I work in PRs: a quality checklist (types, accessibility, performance), clear commit messages, and a self-review before merge. In a team, I suggest simple rules (required CI, PR conventions, definition of “done”).

hero.headline_top: Turn your idea into a product,
hero.headline: Turn your idea into a product. Faster.
hero.subheadline: I’m a freelance engineer building scalable web and mobile products from idea to production using React, Node.js and TypeScript.

mvp.title: MVP Development
mvp.description: Turn your idea into a working product. I build web/mobile MVPs to validate demand and serve your first users quickly.

fullstack.title: Fullstack Product Development
fullstack.description: Complete product development from database architecture to user interface. Scalable solutions built with modern technologies.

scaling.title: Feature Scaling
scaling.description: Expand your existing product with new features and integrations. Maintain code quality while accelerating your roadmap.

audit.title: Technical Audit
audit.description: Comprehensive review of your codebase, architecture, and performance. Get actionable recommendations for improvement.

technicert.name: Technicert
technicert.problem: Certification providers struggled with manual processes and fragmented systems for managing candidates.
technicert.solution: Built a complete SaaS platform for creating, managing, and delivering professional certifications at scale.

afhome.name: AF HOME Services
afhome.problem: Customers needed a clear website to understand shipping options, estimate pricing, and contact the team quickly.
afhome.solution: Built a marketing website showcasing sea and air freight, with mode toggle, pricing calculator, booking form, and WhatsApp contact.
afhome.stack: Marketing website, Responsive, PWA, SEO, Form, Pricing calculator

klikango.name: Klikango
klikango.problem: Local merchants needed a modern way to sell online with integrated payments and delivery tracking.
klikango.solution: Developed a cross-platform commerce solution with real-time order management and payment processing.

expansi.name: Expansi
expansi.problem: Legal professionals lacked efficient tools to network and collaborate across jurisdictions.
expansi.solution: Created a professional networking platform connecting lawyers with business opportunities worldwide.

alafiaplus.name: Alafia+
alafiaplus.problem: Limited access to care, prevention gaps, and a lack of a smooth bridge between patients and healthcare professionals.
alafiaplus.solution: Built a mobile and web health app for Togo to improve access to care, strengthen prevention, and connect patients with professionals (Brevo for emailing, Twilio for video, real-time features via WebSocket).
alafiaplus.stack: React 19, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, React Query, Zustand, React Hook Form, Zod, Axios, AWS S3, Socket.IO, React Router, PWA, Brevo, Twilio

zongopay.name: Zongo Pay
zongopay.problem: Everyday payments remain fragmented and not fully adapted to local realities (mobile money, neighborhood commerce, etc.).
zongopay.solution: Designed a modern, fast, and secure payments solution tailored to Togo’s economic ecosystem, with mobile money integration and optimized flows for merchants and users.
zongopay.stack: React, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Radix UI, React Query, React Hook Form, Zod, React Router, PWA, QR codes, Hono, Prisma, Fedapay, JWT, WebSockets

product.title: Product Mindset
product.description: I think beyond features. Every decision is driven by user value and business impact.

fullstack.title: Fullstack Expertise
fullstack.description: From database design to pixel-perfect UI. One engineer, complete ownership.

experience.title: Production & DevOps
experience.description: Deployments, CI/CD, Docker, monitoring, and best practices to run a stable product in production.

execution.title: Fast Execution
execution.description: Rapid iteration without compromising quality. Ship early, learn fast, improve continuously.

process.title: Process
process.subtitle: From idea to launch in three phases

understand.title: Understand your idea
understand.description: Deep dive into your vision, market, and users. Define the core value proposition and technical requirements.

build.title: Build your product
build.description: Iterative development with regular demos. You stay involved throughout the entire build process.

launch.title: Launch and scale
launch.description: Deploy to production with monitoring and analytics. Support your growth with ongoing improvements.

cta.title: Let’s talk about your product
cta.subtitle: Tell me what you’re building. I’ll reply quickly with a clear plan and next steps.

form.title: Book a call
form.subtitle: Share a few details and I’ll get back to you quickly with a proposal.
